input.prompt:
I need a CollectionView that supports single item selection and
pull-to-refresh. When the user selects an item, I want to navigate
to a detail page. When they pull to refresh, I want to reload data
from my API. Show me the XAML and explain the ViewModel bindings needed.

I need a CollectionView that supports single item selection and
pull-to-refresh. When the user selects an item, I want to navigate
to a detail page. When they pull to refresh, I want to reload data
from my API. Show me the XAML and explain the ViewModel bindings needed.

input.initialPrompt:
I need a CollectionView that supports single item selection and
pull-to-refresh. When the user selects an item, I want to navigate
to a detail page. When they pull to refresh, I want to reload data
from my API. Show me the XAML and explain the ViewModel bindings needed.

data.content:
Here's a complete example:

**XAML (ItemsPage.xaml)**
```xml
<ContentPage xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
             xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml"
             x:Class="MyApp.Views.ItemsPage">

    <CollectionView ItemsSource="{Binding Items}"
                    SelectionMode="Single"
                    SelectedItem="{Binding SelectedItem}"
                    IsRefreshing="{Binding IsRefreshing}"
                    RemainingItemsThreshold="0">

        <!-- Pull-to-refresh via RefreshView wrapper -->
        <CollectionView.Parent>
            <!-- See note below -->
        </CollectionView.Parent>

        <CollectionView.ItemTemplate>
            <DataTemplate>
                <Grid Padding="10" ColumnDefinitions="*,Auto">
                    <Label Text="{Binding Name}" FontSize="16" />
                    <Label Grid.Column="1" Text="{Binding Status}"
                           TextColor="Gray" />
                </Grid>
            </DataTemplate>
        </CollectionView.ItemTemplate>
    </CollectionView>

</ContentPage>
```

Since `CollectionView` doesn't have a built-in refresh, wrap it in a `RefreshView`:

```xml
<RefreshView Command="{Binding RefreshCommand}"
             IsRefreshing="{Binding IsRefreshing}">

    <CollectionView ItemsSource="{Binding Items}"
                    SelectionMode="Single"
                    SelectedItem="{Binding SelectedItem, Mode=TwoWay}">
        <CollectionView.ItemTemplate>
            <DataTemplate>
                <Grid Padding="10" ColumnDefinitions="*,Auto">
                    <Label Text="{Binding Name}" FontSize="16" />
                    <Label Grid.Column="1" Text="{Binding Status}"
                           TextColor="Gray" />
                </Grid>
            </DataTemplate>
        </CollectionView.ItemTemplate>
    </CollectionView>

</RefreshView>
```

**ViewModel (ItemsViewModel.cs)**
```csharp
public partial class ItemsViewModel : ObservableObject
{
    private readonly IApiService _api;
    private readonly INavigationService _nav;

    [ObservableProperty]
    private ObservableCollection<Item> _items = new();

    [ObservableProperty]
    private bool _isRefreshing;

    [ObservableProperty]
    private Item? _selectedItem;

    // Fires when SelectedItem changes — navigate to detail page
    partial void OnSelectedItemChanged(Item? value)
    {
        if (value is null) return;

        _nav.NavigateAsync(nameof(DetailPage), new { Id = value.Id });

        // Clear selection so the user can re-select the same item later
        SelectedItem = null;
    }

    [RelayCommand]
    private async Task RefreshAsync()
    {
        try
        {
            var data = await _api.GetItemsAsync();
            Items = new ObservableCollection<Item>(data);
        }
        finally
        {
            IsRefreshing = false; // stops the spinner
        }
    }
}
```

**Key bindings explained:**

| XAML Property | ViewModel Property | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| `ItemsSource` | `Items` (ObservableCollection) | Drives the list |
| `SelectedItem` (TwoWay) | `SelectedItem` | Triggers navigation via `OnSelectedItemChanged` |
| `RefreshView.Command` | `RefreshCommand` | Auto-generated by `[RelayCommand]` on `RefreshAsync` |
| `RefreshView.IsRefreshing` | `IsRefreshing` | Controls the pull-to-refresh spinner |

**Important details:**
- `SelectedItem` must be **TwoWay** so you can null it out after navigation (allows re-selecting the same row).
- Set `IsRefreshing = false` in a `finally` block so the spinner always stops, even on error.
- This uses the **MVVM Community Toolkit** (`[ObservableProperty]`, `[RelayCommand]`) to eliminate boilerplate.

This is a general knowledge question about MAUI/Xamarin XAML patterns — no codebase to explore. I'll provide the XAML and ViewModel explanation directly.
